Title: New walk-in patient: register, verify identity, start visit and route to triage in one flow
Preconditions: Registration clerk logged in with registration and visit-start privileges; registration desk location selected
Test_Steps: 1. Search for the patient by name and DOB and confirm no match exists | 2. Register a new patient with full demographics | 3. Capture a primary identifier | 4. Start an outpatient visit at the desk | 5. Assign the patient to the Triage queue
Expected_Result: Patient is created once with no duplicate, visit is active, and the patient appears in the Triage queue with correct datetime and location

Title: Returning patient with forgotten ID card is matched on demographics before a duplicate is created
Preconditions: Existing patient record present; registration clerk logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Search by identifier and get no result | 2. Search by name, DOB and gender | 3. Review potential matches presented | 4. Select the existing record rather than creating new
Expected_Result: The existing record is matched and reused, a duplicate is not created, and the clerk is warned of close matches before any create action

Title: Register a newborn with estimated DOB and link to mother record
Preconditions: Mother record exists; clerk has registration privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Register newborn using estimated age in days | 2. Set gender unknown if not yet assigned | 3. Capture relationship to mother | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Newborn is registered with estimated birthdate flag set, relationship to mother is stored, and age displays in days

Title: Reject registration when mandatory demographic fields are blank
Preconditions: Clerk logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Open new patient registration | 2. Leave given name and birthdate blank | 3. Attempt to save
Expected_Result: Save is blocked with field-level validation messages for each missing mandatory field and no record is created

Title: Triage nurse records vitals, chief complaint and acuity then routes to clinician queue
Preconditions: Nurse logged in; patient in Triage queue with active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient from the Triage queue | 2. Start the triage form | 3. Record vitals and chief complaint | 4. Assign acuity level | 5. Save and move to clinician queue
Test_Data: Pulse=88; BP=130/85; Temp=37.2; Acuity=3; Complaint=Cough 3 days
Expected_Result: Triage encounter is saved with vitals and acuity, and the patient moves from Triage to the clinician queue

Title: Abnormal vital triggers a high-acuity prompt (decision table)
Preconditions: Nurse logged in; patient in triage with active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Enter combinations of temperature, systolic BP and SpO2 across normal and critical ranges | 2. Observe acuity recommendation per combination
Expected_Result: System recommends elevated acuity when any value is in the critical range and normal acuity only when all values are normal, per the decision table

Title: Reject impossible vital values at triage
Preconditions: Nurse logged in; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a pulse of 0 | 2. Enter a temperature of 50 C | 3. Attempt to save
Expected_Result: Out-of-range physiologic values are rejected with validation messages and the encounter is not saved

Title: Clinician completes a full consultation: history, diagnosis, orders and plan in one encounter
Preconditions: Clinician logged in; patient in clinician queue with active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient | 2. Record history of presenting illness | 3. Add a coded diagnosis | 4. Place a lab order and a drug order | 5. Write a plan note | 6. Sign the encounter
Expected_Result: Encounter is signed with diagnosis, orders and note all linked to the visit, and orders appear active in their respective queues

Title: Drug order against a documented allergy raises an interaction alert before signing
Preconditions: Clinician logged in; patient has a documented penicillin allergy and active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient with the penicillin allergy | 2. Order Amoxicillin | 3. Attempt to sign the order
Expected_Result: An allergy interaction alert is shown requiring acknowledgement or override with reason before the order can proceed

Title: Allergy override is recorded with reason in the audit trail
Preconditions: Clinician logged in; allergy alert raised on a drug order
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger the allergy alert | 2. Choose to override | 3. Enter an override reason | 4. Sign the order | 5. Review the audit log
Expected_Result: Override action, reason, user and timestamp are written to the audit trail and the order proceeds

Title: Unsigned encounter persists as draft across navigation and re-login
Preconditions: Clinician logged in; active visit with a partially entered encounter
Test_Steps: 1. Enter partial encounter data | 2. Navigate away without signing | 3. Return to the patient | 4. Log out and log back in | 5. Reopen the patient
Expected_Result: Draft content is preserved through navigation and re-login until explicitly signed or discarded

Title: Amend a signed encounter creates a versioned correction not an overwrite
Preconditions: Clinician logged in; a signed encounter exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the signed encounter | 2. Choose amend | 3. Change the diagnosis | 4. Save the amendment | 5. View encounter history
Expected_Result: Original signed content is retained as a prior version, the amendment is recorded with author and timestamp, and history shows both versions

Title: Pharmacist verifies a new prescription, checks interactions and dispenses
Preconditions: Pharmacist logged in; an active drug order is awaiting verification
Test_Steps: 1. Open the pharmacy verification queue | 2. Select the pending order | 3. Review dose, route and frequency | 4. Check interactions | 5. Verify and dispense
Expected_Result: Order moves from pending to dispensed, quantity is decremented from stock, and a dispense record is created

Title: Pharmacist rejects a prescription with an unsafe pediatric dose (boundary)
Preconditions: Pharmacist logged in; pediatric patient with weight recorded; order pending
Test_Steps: 1. Open the pending order for a 12 kg child | 2. Review the weight-based dose | 3. Identify the dose exceeds the max mg per kg | 4. Reject with reason back to prescriber
Expected_Result: The order is flagged as exceeding the safe weight-based maximum and is returned to the prescriber with a documented reason

Title: Partial dispense leaves the balance open and tracked
Preconditions: Pharmacist logged in; verified order for 30 units; only 20 in stock
Test_Steps: 1. Open the verified order | 2. Dispense 20 units | 3. Record a partial dispense | 4. Re-open the order later
Expected_Result: Order state is partially dispensed, a remaining balance of 10 is tracked, and the order remains actionable

Title: Controlled substance dispense requires second-witness sign-off
Preconditions: Pharmacist logged in; order for a scheduled controlled substance; witness user available
Test_Steps: 1. Open the controlled substance order | 2. Attempt to dispense | 3. Provide pharmacist credential | 4. Provide witness credential
Expected_Result: Dispense is blocked until a valid second witness authenticates, and both identities are recorded on the dispense record

Title: Lab tech accepts a specimen, runs the test and posts a result against the order
Preconditions: Lab tech logged in; a lab order with collected specimen exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the lab worklist | 2. Accept the specimen for the order | 3. Enter the numeric result | 4. Validate against the reference range | 5. Release the result
Expected_Result: Result is posted to the order, flagged low against the reference range, and the ordering clinician is notified

Title: Critical lab value triggers mandatory call-back documentation
Preconditions: Lab tech logged in; result entry open
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a result within the critical-low band | 2. Enter a result within normal band | 3. Enter a result within critical-high band | 4. Observe call-back requirement per band
Expected_Result: Critical-low and critical-high results require documented call-back to the clinician before release, while normal results release without call-back

Title: Reject result entry on a cancelled or mismatched specimen
Preconditions: Lab tech logged in; one order cancelled, one specimen barcode mismatched
Test_Steps: 1. Scan a specimen for a cancelled order | 2. Attempt to enter a result | 3. Scan a specimen whose ID does not match the order
Expected_Result: Result entry is blocked for cancelled orders and for specimen-order mismatches with a clear error, preventing wrong-patient results

Title: Amended lab result supersedes prior value and notifies clinician
Preconditions: Lab tech logged in; a released result exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the released result | 2. Amend the value with a correction reason | 3. Release the amendment | 4. View the result history
Expected_Result: Amended result is marked corrected, the prior value is retained in history, and the ordering clinician receives a corrected-result notification

Title: Biller generates a claim from completed visit charges and submits
Preconditions: Biller logged in; completed visit with chargeable services posted
Test_Steps: 1. Open the visit billing summary | 2. Confirm all chargeable services are captured | 3. Map services to billing codes | 4. Generate the claim | 5. Submit
Expected_Result: Claim is generated with correct line items and codes, total matches posted charges, and the claim is queued for submission

Title: Claim is blocked when a required diagnosis-to-procedure link is missing
Preconditions: Biller logged in; visit with a procedure but no supporting diagnosis
Test_Steps: 1. Open the visit with the unlinked procedure | 2. Attempt to generate the claim
Expected_Result: Claim generation is blocked with a message that the procedure requires a supporting diagnosis, preventing a denial

Title: Insurance co-pay and patient balance split is calculated correctly (decision table)
Preconditions: Biller logged in; patient with active insurance and a defined co-pay rule
Test_Steps: 1. Apply claims with covered, partially covered and non-covered services | 2. Observe payer portion and patient balance for each
Expected_Result: Payer and patient split is computed per coverage rule for each scenario and the sum always equals the claim total

Title: Refund on an overpaid invoice posts a negative adjustment and preserves history
Preconditions: Biller logged in; invoice with a recorded overpayment
Test_Steps: 1. Open the overpaid invoice | 2. Initiate a refund for the overpaid amount | 3. Confirm and post | 4. Review invoice ledger
Expected_Result: A refund adjustment is posted, the running balance returns to zero, and the original payment remains visible in the ledger

Title: Admin onboards a new clinician: create user, assign role and scope to location
Preconditions: System admin logged in with user-management privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Create a new user account | 2. Assign the Clinician role | 3. Restrict the user to a single location | 4. Force password change at first login | 5. Save
Expected_Result: User is created with the clinician role, location scope is enforced, and first login forces a password change

Title: Deactivated user immediately loses access and active session is terminated
Preconditions: Admin logged in; target user currently has an active session
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm the target user is logged in elsewhere | 2. Deactivate the user account | 3. Have the target user attempt a privileged action
Expected_Result: Account is disabled, the next request from the existing session is rejected, and the user cannot re-authenticate

Title: Role privilege change takes effect on next login without restart
Preconditions: Admin logged in; a role missing the order-entry privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Add the order-entry privilege to the role | 2. Have a user with that role log out and back in | 3. Attempt order entry
Expected_Result: After re-login the user can perform order entry, confirming privilege propagation without a system restart

Title: Inpatient admission handoff from clinician to ward nurse preserves orders and notes
Preconditions: Clinician and ward nurse accounts exist; patient admitted with standing orders
Test_Steps: 1. Clinician writes admission orders and a handoff note | 2. Patient is admitted to a ward bed | 3. Ward nurse opens the patient on the ward list | 4. Nurse reviews active orders and the handoff note
Expected_Result: Ward nurse sees the active orders, the handoff note and current location, with continuity preserved across the role handoff

Title: Shift change: outgoing nurse signs off and incoming nurse acknowledges pending tasks
Preconditions: Two nurse accounts; ward with pending medication and observation tasks
Test_Steps: 1. Outgoing nurse reviews the pending task list | 2. Records a shift handoff summary | 3. Incoming nurse logs in | 4. Acknowledges the handoff and pending tasks
Expected_Result: Handoff summary is captured, incoming nurse acknowledges, and pending tasks carry forward with no loss across the shift boundary

Title: Full appointment lifecycle: book, check-in, convert to visit, complete, no-show variant
Preconditions: Scheduler and clinician accounts; provider with available slots
Test_Steps: 1. Book an appointment in an open slot | 2. Check the patient in on arrival | 3. Convert the appointment to an active visit | 4. Complete after the encounter | 5. In a parallel case mark a separate appointment as no-show
Expected_Result: Each appointment transitions through valid states only, a no-show is recorded distinctly, and completed appointments link to their visit

Title: Double-booking the same provider slot is prevented
Preconditions: Scheduler logged in; provider slot already booked
Test_Steps: 1. Open the provider schedule | 2. Attempt to book a second appointment in the occupied slot
Expected_Result: Booking is rejected with a slot-conflict message unless overbooking is explicitly permitted for that provider

Title: Cancellation within the late-cancel window flags a fee per policy (decision table)
Preconditions: Scheduler logged in; cancellation policy configured with a 24-hour window
Test_Steps: 1. Cancel an appointment more than 24 hours out | 2. Cancel one within 24 hours | 3. Cancel a same-day appointment | 4. Observe fee flag per case
Expected_Result: No fee is flagged outside the window, a late-cancel fee is flagged inside the window, and policy is applied consistently

Title: Outbound referral to a specialist creates a referral record with reason and packet
Preconditions: Clinician logged in; specialty target configured
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient | 2. Create an outbound referral | 3. Select the specialty and reason | 4. Attach the clinical summary | 5. Submit
Expected_Result: Referral is created with reason, target specialty and an attached summary, and shows as pending acceptance

Title: Referral status round-trip: accepted, scheduled, completed back to referrer
Preconditions: Referral created and pending; specialist account exists
Test_Steps: 1. Specialist accepts the referral | 2. Schedules the patient | 3. Completes the consult and writes back findings | 4. Referrer reviews the closed-loop status
Expected_Result: Referral progresses through accepted, scheduled and completed, and findings are returned to the original referrer closing the loop

Title: Enroll patient in a chronic care program and record an initial program visit
Preconditions: Clinician logged in; a care program is configured
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient | 2. Enroll in the chronic care program | 3. Set the enrollment date and initial state | 4. Record the first program encounter
Expected_Result: Enrollment is created with start date and state, and the initial program encounter is linked to the enrollment

Title: Program state transitions follow the allowed workflow only
Preconditions: Clinician logged in; patient enrolled with state Active
Test_Steps: 1. Move the patient from Active to On Treatment | 2. Attempt an invalid jump to a terminal state and back | 3. Move to a valid completion state
Expected_Result: Only configured state transitions are allowed, invalid transitions are blocked, and each state change is dated and audited

Title: Merge two duplicate patient records preserving all clinical data
Preconditions: Admin with merge privilege; two confirmed duplicate records of the same person
Test_Steps: 1. Identify the two duplicate records | 2. Select the surviving (preferred) record | 3. Review observations, visits and orders to be merged | 4. Confirm the merge
Expected_Result: Both records are merged into the survivor, no encounters or orders are lost, and the non-survivor identifier resolves to the survivor

Title: Merge is blocked or warns when records have conflicting active diagnoses
Preconditions: Admin with merge privilege; two records with conflicting active problems
Test_Steps: 1. Select two records with conflicting active diagnoses | 2. Initiate the merge | 3. Review the conflict warning
Expected_Result: A conflict warning is presented requiring explicit reviewer confirmation before the merge proceeds, preventing silent data corruption

Title: Patient consent restriction blocks data sharing in an export
Preconditions: Privacy officer logged in; patient with a recorded sensitive-data restriction
Test_Steps: 1. Apply a consent restriction on the patient | 2. Run a data export that would include the patient | 3. Inspect the export contents
Expected_Result: Restricted data is excluded or masked from the export per the consent directive and the suppression is logged

Title: Break-the-glass emergency access is logged and reviewable
Preconditions: Clinician logged in; patient outside the clinician's normal care scope
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to open a restricted patient | 2. Invoke break-the-glass with a reason | 3. Access the record | 4. Privacy officer reviews the access log
Expected_Result: Access is granted after reason capture, and the break-the-glass event with user, reason and timestamp is written to an immutable audit log

Title: Inbound ADT admit message creates a visit reconciled to the correct patient
Preconditions: HL7 interface engine connected; patient exists with a known MRN
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ADT A01 admit message with the patient MRN | 2. Observe patient match | 3. Verify the visit and location created
Expected_Result: The admit is matched to the existing patient by MRN, a visit is created at the stated location, and no duplicate patient is generated

Title: Inbound ORU result message posts to the matching lab order
Preconditions: HL7 interface connected; an outstanding lab order exists with a placer order number
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ORU R01 result referencing the placer order number | 2. Observe order matching | 3. Verify the result and units
Expected_Result: The result is attached to the correct order with value, units and reference range, and an unmatched result is routed to an exception queue

Title: Outbound FHIR encounter is exported with correct references and codes
Preconditions: FHIR API enabled; a signed encounter exists
Test_Steps: 1. Request the Encounter resource via the FHIR API | 2. Validate subject, participant and period references | 3. Validate diagnosis coding
Expected_Result: Encounter resource resolves valid references to Patient and Practitioner, carries the correct period, and uses recognized diagnosis code systems

Title: FHIR bundle posted with an invalid reference is rejected atomically
Preconditions: FHIR API enabled with transaction bundle support
Test_Steps: 1. Post a transaction bundle where one entry references a non-existent patient | 2. Observe the response
Expected_Result: The whole transaction bundle is rejected with an operation outcome, and no partial resources are persisted

Title: Scheduled video consult: join, document encounter, e-prescribe
Preconditions: Clinician and patient enrolled for a tele-visit; appointment booked
Test_Steps: 1. Clinician joins the video session at appointment time | 2. Confirms patient identity | 3. Documents the encounter | 4. E-prescribes and ends the session
Expected_Result: The tele-encounter is documented and linked to the appointment, the prescription is generated, and session start and end are recorded

Title: Tele-visit join is blocked before identity verification
Preconditions: Clinician in a tele-session lobby
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to start documentation before verifying patient identity | 2. Observe the guard | 3. Verify identity then proceed
Expected_Result: Documentation and prescribing are blocked until patient identity is verified, then proceed normally

Title: Antenatal visit captures gestational age, fetal heart rate and risk flag
Preconditions: Clinician logged in; pregnant patient enrolled in antenatal care
Test_Steps: 1. Open the antenatal patient | 2. Record gestational age and fetal heart rate | 3. Capture danger signs | 4. Set the risk classification
Expected_Result: Antenatal observations are saved, the risk classification is set, and high-risk flags surface on the patient summary

Title: Pediatric growth measurement plots against age and flags malnutrition (boundary)
Preconditions: Clinician logged in; pediatric patient with prior growth points
Test_Steps: 1. Record weight and height for the child | 2. Compute weight-for-age | 3. Observe the z-score band at the boundary thresholds
Expected_Result: Growth value plots on the chart, the z-score band is computed, and a value at or below the severe threshold flags malnutrition

Title: Unidentified emergency patient registered as John Doe then reconciled later
Preconditions: ED clerk logged in; unidentified critical patient arrives
Test_Steps: 1. Register an unidentified patient with a temporary alias | 2. Start an emergency visit | 3. Provide care under the alias | 4. Later reconcile to the real identity
Expected_Result: Care is delivered under the temporary identity, and on reconciliation all encounters and orders carry forward to the verified identity without loss

Title: ED order set fires standard panel and the right alerts (pairwise)
Preconditions: ED clinician logged in; chest-pain order set configured
Test_Steps: 1. Apply the chest-pain order set across pairwise combinations of patient sex, age band and allergy presence | 2. Observe orders and alerts generated
Expected_Result: Each combination produces the correct order set members and surfaces allergy or age-appropriate alerts without missing or extra orders

Title: Discharge a patient: medication reconciliation, summary, follow-up and bed release
Preconditions: Clinician and nurse accounts; admitted patient ready for discharge
Test_Steps: 1. Reconcile inpatient and home medications | 2. Generate the discharge summary | 3. Schedule a follow-up appointment | 4. Discharge and release the bed
Expected_Result: Discharge summary captures reconciled meds and diagnoses, the follow-up is booked, the visit is closed, and the bed is released to available

Title: Discharge blocked while critical orders or unresulted labs remain open
Preconditions: Clinician logged in; admitted patient with an open critical lab order
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to discharge the patient | 2. Observe the open-orders guard | 3. Resolve or acknowledge the open items | 4. Retry discharge
Expected_Result: Discharge is blocked or warns while critical open orders exist, requiring resolution or explicit clinician acknowledgement before completion

Title: Concurrent edits to the same encounter by two clinicians are reconciled safely
Preconditions: Two clinician sessions; same encounter open in both
Test_Steps: 1. Both users open the same encounter | 2. User A edits and saves | 3. User B edits and attempts to save the stale copy
Expected_Result: User B is prevented from silently overwriting User A's change via optimistic locking and is prompted to reload and reconcile

Title: Voided observation is retained for audit and excluded from active views
Preconditions: Clinician logged in; an erroneous observation recorded
Test_Steps: 1. Void the observation with a reason | 2. Confirm it disappears from the active dashboard | 3. Query the audit or void history
Expected_Result: Voided observation is hidden from active clinical views but remains recoverable in the audit history with reason, user and timestamp

Title: Department manager runs a daily activity report scoped to their location only
Preconditions: Manager logged in with location-scoped reporting privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Open the daily activity report | 2. Run for the current date | 3. Inspect the patient and encounter rows | 4. Attempt to widen the scope to another location
Expected_Result: Report returns only the manager's location data, totals reconcile to source encounters, and cross-location data is not accessible

Title: Aggregate report suppresses small cells to protect privacy
Preconditions: Analyst logged in; aggregate report with small subgroup counts
Test_Steps: 1. Run an aggregate report containing a subgroup with a count below the suppression threshold | 2. Inspect the small-count cells
Expected_Result: Cells below the small-count threshold are suppressed or masked to prevent re-identification while totals remain

Title: Triage nurse completes vitals entry using keyboard only
Preconditions: Nurse logged in; triage form open; keyboard-only operation
Test_Steps: 1. Tab through all vitals fields in logical order | 2. Enter values without a mouse | 3. Trigger save via keyboard | 4. Confirm focus moves to the confirmation
Expected_Result: All fields are reachable and operable by keyboard in a logical order, focus is visible, and the encounter saves without mouse use

Title: Critical allergy alert is announced by a screen reader
Preconditions: Clinician logged in; screen reader active; allergy alert will trigger
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger the allergy interaction alert | 2. Confirm the screen reader announces the alert text and role | 3. Confirm the alert can be acknowledged via keyboard
Expected_Result: The allergy alert is announced as an alert with its full text and is acknowledgeable by keyboard, ensuring no safety-critical alert is missed

Title: Idle session timeout protects an open chart at an unattended workstation
Preconditions: Clinician logged in with a patient chart open; idle timeout configured
Test_Steps: 1. Open a patient chart | 2. Leave the workstation idle past the timeout | 3. Return and attempt to interact
Expected_Result: The session locks or expires after the configured idle period and re-authentication is required before any further access to PHI

Title: Wrong-patient guard: order placement on the wrong open chart is interrupted
Preconditions: Clinician logged in; two patient charts opened in sequence
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient A then open patient B | 2. Begin an order intending patient A but on patient B's chart | 3. Observe the patient-confirmation step
Expected_Result: An active patient indicator and confirmation step make the wrong-patient context evident before the order commits, reducing wrong-patient errors

Title: Same UAT consult flow executes on the adapter against an alternate backend
Preconditions: Resource Adapter Layer configured against an alternate FHIR backend; tester credentials valid
Test_Steps: 1. Run the core consult flow (open patient, diagnosis, order) through the adapter | 2. Verify resources land in the alternate backend | 3. Compare field mapping to the OpenMRS run
Expected_Result: The consult flow completes against the alternate backend with equivalent resources and field mappings, confirming portability of the scenario

Title: Day-in-the-life exploratory sweep of the outpatient journey for friction and defects
Preconditions: Full outpatient environment available; charter and time-box defined
Test_Steps: 1. Time-box a session following one patient end to end | 2. Probe error handling, back-button, refresh and concurrent tabs | 3. Note any data loss, confusing states or safety gaps | 4. Log findings
Expected_Result: Exploratory findings are documented with reproduction notes and severity, surfacing friction and defects not covered by scripted cases

Title: Problem list reconciliation: resolve, reactivate and de-duplicate conditions
Preconditions: Clinician logged in; patient with several active and resolved problems
Test_Steps: 1. Mark an active problem resolved with a date | 2. Reactivate a previously resolved problem | 3. Identify and merge a duplicate condition | 4. Review the resulting problem list
Expected_Result: Each transition is dated and reflected on the problem list, duplicates collapse to one entry, and history of state changes is retained

Title: Claim denial is recorded and a corrected resubmission is tracked
Preconditions: Biller logged in; a submitted claim returned as denied
Test_Steps: 1. Record the denial with the payer reason code | 2. Correct the offending line item | 3. Resubmit as a corrected claim | 4. Track the resubmission status
Expected_Result: The denial and reason are recorded, the corrected claim links to the original, and the resubmission status is tracked through to adjudication
